questions.question: Find the odd one
Find the next term in the series. G, E, B, Z, W, U, R, ?, ?
questions.explanation: G _ E _ _ B _ Z _ _ W _ U _ _ R _ P _ _ M

questions.question: In the given equation, LHS = 2 X RHS, only when we interchange numbers _________ on same side. 10 X 6 ÷ 3 - 12 + 18 = 3 + 1 X 6 - 8 ÷ 4
questions.explanation: LHS = 10 X 6 ÷ 3 - 12 + 18 = 26 RHS = 3 + 1 X 6 - 8 ÷ 4 = 7 If 12 and 18 are interchanged in LHS, we get, LHS = 10 X 6 ÷ 3 - 18 + 12 = 14 Hence, LHS = 2 X 7= 2 X RHS

questions.question: In the following question select the odd word pair from the given alternatives.
questions.explanation: Forest should have trees. Jungle and forest are synonyms.

questions.question: Find the minimum number of straight lines required to draw the following figure.
questions.explanation: Lines = AB, BC, CD, DE, EF, FG, GH, HA, AF, BE, CG, DH, HI and JC = 14.

questions.question: Arrange the words given below in a meaningful sequence. 1. Resolution 2. Complaint 3. Customer Care 4. Service 5. Breakdown
questions.explanation: The correct sequence is: 53241. Breakdown, customer care, complaint, service, resolution.

questions.question: In a certain code language 'FIERCE' is coded as 'FDSGKH'. How is 'BREAKS' coded in that language?
questions.explanation: The word is reversed → S K A E R B, then coded as follows- S+1=T K+1=L A+1=B E+2=G R+2=T B+2=D

questions.question: In the following question, select the number which can be placed at the sign of question mark (?) from the given alternatives. 6121824 481216 115216 ?28424
questions.explanation: In each row, the logic is followed - 12+18 - 6 = 24 8+12 - 4 = 16 15+2 - 1 = 16 Similarly, 28 + 4 - (?) = 24 (?) = 8

questions.question: Find out from amongst the four alternatives as to how the pattern would appear on the sheet when the sheet is folded at the dotted line and is punched as given. a) b) c) d)
questions.explanation: The page would look like that in option b

questions.question: In each of the following questions, select a figure from amongst the four alternatives, which when placed in the blank space of figure (X) would complete the pattern. Identify the figure that completes the pattern. a) b) c) d)

Which of the following will be the mirror image of the given question figure, if the mirror is placed along the line MN. Choose the correct mirror image among (I), (II), (III) and (IV) given below the question figure.
From the given figures (I), (II), (III) and (IV), select the one in which question figure (X) is hidden/embedded.
Select the set of numbers that is not similar to the following set of numbers. (60, 12, 3)
questions.explanation: Except option (c), all follow the logic - First number/5 = second number and, sum of digits of second number = third number. 60/5 = 12 and 1+2=3 Similarly, 930/5=186 and 1+8+6=15 155/5=31 and 3+1=4 85/5=17 and 1+7=8 But, 145/5=29 and 2+9=11

questions.question: Identify the diagram that best represents the relationship among the given classes. Mirror, lens, spectacles
questions.explanation: All spectacles have lens, but mirror is a different entity.

questions.question: Select the figure that will come next in the following series.
questions.explanation: The shaded part of triangle varies in alternate steps. Also, the shaded portion of small rectangle is varies its position alternately. Remaining parts are rotated as follows - (arrows indicate clockwise/anticlockwise movement of the lines)

questions.question: In the following question, select the related letters from the given alternatives. AKD : CIB ∷ ? : HCU
questions.explanation: A+2=C K-2=I D-2=B Similarly, since, we are given the later part i.e. HCU, we get, it is derived from - H-2=F C+2=E U+2=W Thus, FEW becomes HCU.

questions.question: Select the word-pair in which two words are related in the same way as are the following word-pair. Glass: brittle
questions.explanation: Here, a thing is given with its adjective such as glass is brittle. Similarly, blade is sharp.

questions.question: If P # Q means P is father Q; P * Q means P is mother of Q; P @ Q means P is brother of Q and P $ Q means P is sister of Q. Which of the following represents that M is Uncle of Q?
questions.explanation: M @ N @ O # P $ Q From this representation, M is the uncle of Q.

questions.question: Find the next term in the series. 100, 130, 164, 202, 244, ?

U's house is to the south of S's house. V and T's house are in the same lane of S where T's house is to the north-east of U's house. If K's house is to the west of M's house, which is to the south of T's house then what is the direction of K's house with respect to S's house?
questions.explanation: We do not know how far K's house is to the west of M's house. Similarly for others we don't know the exact distances. So direction can't be determined.

questions.question: If '%' means '÷', '#' means '+', '!' means 'x ' and '@' means '-', then what will be the value of, 60#84%7@6!4#24%2@30
From the given alternative words, select the word which cannot be formed using the letters of the given word 'curiosity'?
questions.explanation: The word CURIOUS cannot be formed as there are two U's present in it while the given word has only one.

questions.question: Arun, Prakash, Ravi, Nitin and Vipin are sitting on a bench facing north. Arun is sitting next to Prakash, Ravi is sitting next to Nitin, Nitin is not sitting with Vipin, who is sitting on the left end of the bench. Arun is sitting to the right of Prakash and Vipin. Arun and Ravi are sitting together. Ravi is sitting on the second place from the right. Arun is sitting with:
questions.explanation: The order of seats that we derive from the above conditions is: Vipin-Prakash-Arun-Ravi-Nitin Therefore, the correct answer is option a.

questions.question: Arrange the following words as per the order in dictionary. 1. Involuntary 2. Investiture 3. Invocation 4. Inveterate 5. Invigilate
questions.explanation: This is the correct form as per the order in dictionary. Investiture > Inveterate > Invigilate > Invocation > Involuntary

questions.question: In each question below are three statements followed by three conclusions numbered I, II and III. You have to take the three given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance from commonly known facts and then decide which of the given conclusions logically follows from the three given statements disregarding commonly known facts. Statements: All dams are bikes. Some bikes are brakes. All brakes are umpires. Conclusions: I. Some umpires are dams. II. Some brakes are dams. III. Some umpires are bikes.
questions.explanation: Only III follows
